evidence: exp ref-slew-dense-long-sweep tests idea
dense+long Simulink switching-level sweep shows objective-dependent best T_slew values under base and settling-aware scores.
Offline policy evaluation shows base-score and settling-aware objectives select different T_slew schedules.
exp ref-slew-scheduler-policy-eval tests idea
exp ai-supervisor-training-interface tests idea
Derived Simulink delayed-reference table-supervisor validation tests the PIS-IEK AI scheduling interface.
At tau_AI=5us, alpha=0.05 and alpha=0.10 tables optimize different scores and outperform fixed slopes under their corresponding objectives.
Derived Simulink delayed-reference runs across tau_AI=0.5/1/2/5us test the PIS-IEK delay-aware scheduling interface.
Best table policy changes with both objective weight and parameter-commit delay.
R022 score-prediction supervisor uses PIS-IEK delay coordinates and existing delayed-reference switching data.
Qualified support: interpretable supervisor beats fixed slopes and approximates strategy ranking, but does not stably beat the strong zero-delay objective table.
R023 continuous T_slew landscape post-processes dense+long sweep to estimate fine-sweep candidates and near-optimal bands.
Qualified support: continuous T_slew has small estimated gains and should be treated as safe-band smoothing, not proven global optimum.
R024 local Simulink fine sweep tests R023 continuous T_slew candidates and mode-aware scheduling boundaries.
Qualified support: fine sweep supports small local gains from finer T_slew grids but shows skip/reentry non-smoothness; interpolated candidates are not point-optimal proofs.
R025 uses dense+long+fine rows to test smooth vs mode-aware continuous T_slew score surrogates and safety projection.
Qualified support: mode-aware safety projection reduces offline regret relative to naked continuous minimization, but remains post-processing rather than AI-in-loop or hardware validation.
R026 tests whether posterior mode-aware T_slew indicators can be converted into a deployable calibrated r_hat(z,T_slew) risk proxy for supervisor safety projection.

Qualified support: R026 calibrated risk proxy mean regret 0.119 beats dense_long table 0.163 and naked continuous 0.654, but remains weaker than posterior mode-aware projection 0.064; supports deployable proxy interface, not hardware AI-in-loop.
R027 converts the deployable risk proxy into a derived Simulink table-in-loop validation matrix and MATLAB runner.

Qualified support: R027 completes 48 priority switching cases; dense-long and posterior mean switching regret are 0.025, calibrated proxy is 0.283, so the proxy interface is executable but needs safety-bound re-calibration.
R028 uses completed R027 priority switching results to recalibrate B_epsilon(z,r_hat,tau_AI) for the deployable supervisory proxy interface.
R028 dense-anchor projection reduces old proxy priority mean switching regret from 0.283 to 0.025, tying dense-long table on the stress set; guarded rule is held-out validation candidate.
R029 executes 21 held-out derived Simulink cases to test whether the R028 guarded proxy overfits the R027 priority stress contexts.
R029 supports the 10A tau>=2us 34us guard but revises the near0A fixed 35us guard into a 30-38us local safety band.
R030 post-processes R027/R028/R029 evidence into a refined local band policy and a dense-anchor challenge plan.
R030 turns R029's 10A delay guard and near0A 30-38us correction into a deployable-style local band rule while preserving claim boundaries.
R031 uses R030 dense-anchor challenge negative samples to tighten the B_epsilon^sw supervisory projection interface.
R031 blocks high-risk proxy overrides, keeps only calibrated near-tie candidates, and emits a 22-row minimal held-out validation plan.
R032 post-processes R031 held-out results into a short-horizon risk interface and delay-aware B_epsilon^sw projection.
R032 upgrades R031 delay-aware local bands into q_phi/r_hat/B_epsilon^sw interface and emits a 31-row next validation plan.
R033 validates R032 delay-band rules with 31 derived-Simulink delayed-reference cases.
R033 corrects R032 plant-commit boundaries for 10A near-tie, 20A base, and 20A score_settle005 transition pocket.
R034 turns R033 boundary evidence into a deployable risk predictor prototype and validation plan.
R034 encodes R033 near-tie, objective-probe, and transition-pocket findings into q_phi/r_hat/B_epsilon rules.
R034 partial derived-Simulink validation tests the transition pocket edges at tau=1.25us and tau=1.75us.
Partial validation revises fixed 50us pocket into a moving transition ridge hypothesis.
R034 full validation completes 20 derived-Simulink transition-pocket cases.
Full validation adds tau=1.0us and tau=2.0us, revising moving ridge into folded band.
R035 revises the R034 folded sequence into a candidate generator plus dense-inclusive B_epsilon^sw projection, keeping tau_AI=2us on 30us fallback.
exp folded-band-projection-r035 tests idea
